name: Brag House Holdings, Inc.
business: Brag House operates a media-tech platform designed for casual college gamers to drive community-driven gaming experiences, generating revenue primarily from advertising and sponsorship fees related to gaming tournaments for corporate sponsors.
sellsTo: corporate sponsors and brands seeking access to Gen Z demographic

exposures.sentence: Brag House is a mission-driven organization that utilizes a diversified business strategy to operate a media-tech platform designed for casual college gamers to drive community-driven gaming experiences anchored in the college sports culture, while creating authentic pathways for brands to connect with our Gen Z audience.

The goal is to develop a proprietary machine learning-based software-as-a-service (SaaS) platform designed to offer anonymized predictive data insights into Gen Z behavior for brand clients to create enhanced, personalized and effective marketing campaigns, which will validate our marketing and data strategy for reaching college-aged Gen Z gamers.

catalysts.event: Merger with House of Doge expected to close
catalysts.sentence: The Company expects the Merger to be finalized by May 29, 2026, pending satisfaction of all closing conditions.

catalysts.event: Beta version of proprietary machine learning data insights platform ready
catalysts.sentence: We began development of this platform in March 2025 and expect to have a beta version ready by the third quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global video gaming revenues were $224 billion in 2024, expected to grow to nearly $300 billion in 2029 at a compound annual growth rate of 5.7%.
tamClaims.sentence: According to PWC, global video gaming revenues were $224 billion in 2024, with the industry expected to grow to nearly $300 billion in 2029 at a compound annual growth rate of 5.7%.

tamClaims.claim: Advertising in video games is projected to rise from 32.8% of total gaming revenue in 2024 to 38.5% in 2029.
tamClaims.sentence: PWC reports that advertising in video games is a high-growth area, rising from 32.8% of total gaming revenue in 2024 to a projected 38.5% in 2029.

tamClaims.claim: Digital formats are expected to account for 80% of overall global advertising revenue in 2029, up from 72% in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: According to PricewaterhouseCoopers (PWC), digital formats are expected to account for 80% of overall global advertising revenue in 2029 (up from 72% in 2024), with new technologies including AI and hyper-personalization expected to drive this growth.

tamClaims.claim: Gaming viewership exceeded 32.5 billion hours globally in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Net Influencer, gaming viewership exceeded globally 32.5 billion hours of watched content by spectators worldwide in 2024;
